questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm?
options: A) To minimize training time
B) To minimize the difference between predicted and actual values
C) To find the k most similar instances
D) To maximize the similarity between instances
questions.explanation: The Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm aims to find the k most similar instances to the target instance, with weights assigned to each similarity measure.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key advantage of the Nearest-Neighbor learning algorithm?
options: A) High precision but low recall
B) High recall but low precision
C) Robustness to noise in the data
D) Ability to handle high-dimensional feature spaces
questions.explanation: The Nearest-Neighbor learning algorithm is robust to noise in the data, as it simply finds the nearest instance in the training set.

questions.question: What is the main difference between the Nearest-Neighbor and Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithms?
options: A) The use of weighted averages
B) The use of different distance metrics
C) The handling of noisy data
D) The number of nearest neighbors considered
questions.explanation: The main difference between the Nearest-Neighbor and Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithms is that the latter uses weighted averages to compute the similarity between instances.

questions.question: Which of the following is a potential limitation of the Nearest-Neighbor algorithm?
options: A) High computational complexity
B) Low accuracy in high-dimensional spaces
C) Inability to handle missing values
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: The Nearest-Neighbor algorithm can suffer from high computational complexity and low accuracy in high-dimensional spaces.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'k' parameter in the Nearest-Neighbor algorithm?
options: A) To control the trade-off between precision and recall
B) To specify the number of nearest neighbors to consider
C) To handle missing values
D) To normalize the feature values
questions.explanation: The 'k' parameter in the Nearest-Neighbor algorithm specifies the number of nearest neighbors to consider when making predictions.
